The 25th-75th percentile range of $76-$108 indicates moderate consensus, though tail risks extend to $68 (10th percentile) and $119 (90th percentile).

By end-June 2026, prediction markets shift decisively bearish with a median of $73/bbl, implying a 16.7% decline from spot despite light volume of $655K across 12 markets. The wide interquartile range ($65-$128) and extreme 90th percentile at $164 suggest significant uncertainty, with markets pricing in meaningful downside risk over the two-month forward period.
